papers.title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation
papers.abstract: AutoGen is an open-source framework that allows developers to build LLM applications via multiple agents that can converse with each other to accomplish tasks. AutoGen agents are customizable, conversable, and can operate in various modes that employ combinations of LLMs, human inputs, and tools. Using AutoGen, developers can also flexibly define agent interaction behaviors. Both natural language and computer code can be used to program flexible conversation patterns for different applications.
key_contributions: Integration of LLMs, humans, and tools
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/AutoGen_Multi_Agent_Conversation.pdf

papers.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
papers.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have revolutionized AI, but are constrained by limited context windows, hindering their utility in tasks like extended conversations and document analysis. To enable using context beyond limited context windows, we propose virtual context management, a technique drawing inspiration from hierarchical memory systems in traditional operating systems that provide the appearance of large memory resources through data movement between fast and slow memory.
key_contributions: Virtual context management inspired by OS memory hierarchies
papers.local_path: 论文收集/03_LLM工具与智能体记忆/MemGPT_LLM_as_OS.pdf

papers.title: Data Interpreter: An LLM Agent For Data Science
papers.abstract: Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents have shown effectiveness across many applications. However, their use in data science scenarios requiring solving long-term interconnected tasks, dynamic data adjustments and domain expertise remains challenging. We present Data Interpreter, an LLM-based agent designed to automatically solve various data science problems end-to-end with hierarchical graph modeling and programmable node generation.
key_contributions: Hierarchical graph modeling for complex data science tasks
Programmable node generation for code refinement
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/Data_Interpreter_Data_Science.pdf

papers.title: Reflective Multi-Agent Collaboration based on Large Language Models
papers.abstract: We propose a novel framework, named COPPER, to enhance the collaborative capabilities of LLM-based agents with the self-reflection mechanism. Our framework enables agents to learn from their interactions and improve their collaboration strategies through reflective feedback loops.
key_contributions: COPPER framework for enhanced agent cooperation
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/COPPER_Reflective_Multi_Agent.pdf

papers.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration Mechanisms: A Survey of LLMs
papers.abstract: These LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MASs) enable groups of intelligent agents to coordinate and solve complex tasks collectively at scale, transitioning from isolated models to collaboration-centric approaches. This work provides an extensive survey of the collaborative aspect of MASs and introduces an extensible framework to guide future research.
key_contributions: Comprehensive survey of LLM-based multi-agent systems
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/Survey_Multi_Agent_Collaboration.pdf

papers.title: MultiAgentBench: Evaluating the Collaboration and Competition of LLM Agents
papers.abstract: We introduce MultiAgentBench, a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate LLM-based multi-agent systems across diverse, interactive scenarios. Our framework measures not only task completion but also the quality of collaboration and competition using novel, milestone-based key performance indicators.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/MultiAgentBench_Evaluation.pdf

papers.title: Synthius-Mem: Brain-Inspired Hallucination-Resistant Persona Memory
papers.abstract: Providing AI agents with reliable long-term memory that does not hallucinate remains an open problem. We present Synthius-Mem, a brain-inspired structured persona memory system that takes a fundamentally different approach. Instead of retrieving what was said, Synthius-Mem extracts what is known about the person.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/03_LLM工具与智能体记忆/Synthius_Mem_Persona_Memory.pdf

papers.title: RoMem: Continuous Phase Rotation for Temporal Knowledge Graphs and Agentic Memory
papers.abstract: Structured memory representations such as knowledge graphs are central to autonomous agents and other long-lived systems. We introduce RoMem, a drop-in temporal knowledge graph module for structured memory systems, applicable to agentic memory and beyond.
key_contributions: Semantic Speed Gate for volatility scoring
papers.local_path: 论文收集/03_LLM工具与智能体记忆/RoMem_Temporal_Memory.pdf

papers.title: Escaping the Context Bottleneck: Active Context Curation for LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) struggle with long-horizon tasks due to the context bottleneck and the lost-in-the-middle phenomenon. We introduce a symbiotic framework that decouples context management from task execution with a ContextCurator model.
key_contributions: Active context curation via reinforcement learning
papers.local_path: 论文收集/03_LLM工具与智能体记忆/Context_Curation_LLMAgents.pdf

papers.title: ClawGUI: A Unified Framework for Training, Evaluating, and Deploying GUI Agents
papers.abstract: GUI agents drive applications through visual interfaces. We present ClawGUI with RL training infrastructure, standardized evaluation, and deployment with persistent personalized memory.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/03_LLM工具与智能体记忆/ClawGUI_GUI_Agents.pdf

papers.title: Think-Augmented Function Calling: Improving LLM Parameter Accuracy
papers.abstract: We propose Think-Augmented Function Calling (TAFC), a novel framework that enhances function calling accuracy through explicit reasoning at both function and parameter levels. Our method introduces a universal think parameter augmentation that enables models to articulate their decision-making process.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/04_AI智能体与机器人协作2025/TAFC_Function_Calling.pdf

papers.title: ToolRLA: Multiplicative Reward Decomposition for Tool-Integrated Agents
papers.abstract: Tool-integrated agents that interleave reasoning with API calls are promising for complex tasks. We present ToolRLA, a three-stage post-training pipeline for domain-specific tool agents with fine-grained reward decomposition.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/04_AI智能体与机器人协作2025/ToolRLA_Tool_Integration.pdf

papers.title: AgentHER: Hindsight Experience Replay for LLM Agent Trajectory Relabeling
papers.abstract: We introduce AgentHER, a framework that recovers lost training signal by adapting Hindsight Experience Replay to natural-language agent trajectories for offline data augmentation. A trajectory that fails goal A is often a correct demonstration for some achievable alternative goal B.
key_contributions: Hindsight Experience Replay for LLM agents
papers.local_path: 论文收集/04_AI智能体与机器人协作2025/AgentHER_HER_LLMAgents.pdf

papers.title: ClawGuard: A Runtime Security Framework for Tool-Augmented LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Tool-augmented LLM agents remain vulnerable to indirect prompt injection. We introduce ClawGuard, a runtime security framework that enforces user-confirmed rule sets at every tool-call boundary.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/04_AI智能体与机器人协作2025/ClawGuard_Security_Tool_Agents.pdf

papers.title: Small Language Models for Efficient Agentic Tool Calling
papers.abstract: We investigate the feasibility of replacing LLM-driven workflows with optimized SLMs. Experimental results demonstrate that our fine-tuned SLM achieves 77.55% pass rate on ToolBench evaluation, significantly outperforming baseline models.
key_contributions: Small language models for tool calling
papers.local_path: 论文收集/04_AI智能体与机器人协作2025/SLM_Tool_Calling.pdf

papers.title: Collaborative Multi-Agent Scripts Generation for Imperfect-Information Reasoning
papers.abstract: We propose a collaborative multi-agent framework for evaluating and synthesizing high-quality, role-driven multiplayer game scripts. Our system generates rich multimodal contexts through coordinated agent interactions.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/Collaborative_Multi_Agent_Scripts.pdf

papers.title: Evaluating Cooperation in LLM Social Groups through Elected Leadership
papers.abstract: We present an open-source framework that simulates leadership through elected personas and candidate-driven agendas. Experiments demonstrate that elected leadership improves social welfare scores by 55.4% and survival time by 128.6%.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/LLM_Social_Groups_Leadership.pdf

papers.title: PAC-Bench: Evaluating Multi-Agent Collaboration under Privacy Constraints
papers.abstract: We present PAC-Bench, a benchmark for systematic evaluation of multi-agent collaboration under privacy constraints. Privacy constraints substantially degrade collaboration performance and make outcomes depend more on the initiating agent than the partner.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/PAC_Bench_Privacy_Collaboration.pdf

papers.title: CodeTracer: Towards Traceable Agent States
papers.abstract: Code agents are advancing rapidly, but debugging them is increasingly difficult. We present CodeTracer, a tracing architecture that parses heterogeneous run artifacts and reconstructs full state transition history as a hierarchical trace tree with persistent memory.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/03_LLM工具与智能体记忆/CodeTracer_Traceable_Agents.pdf

papers.title: PRIME: Proactive Reasoning via Iterative Memory Evolution
papers.abstract: We introduce PRIME (Proactive Reasoning via Iterative Memory Evolution), a gradient-free learning framework that enables continuous agent evolvement through explicit experience accumulation. PRIME distills multi-turn interaction trajectories into structured experiences organized across three semantic zones.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/03_LLM工具与智能体记忆/PRIME_Proactive_Reasoning.pdf

papers.title: Agentic Aggregation for Parallel Scaling of Long-Horizon Agentic Tasks
papers.abstract: We propose AggAgent, an aggregation agent that treats parallel trajectories as an environment. We equip it with lightweight tools to inspect candidate solutions and search across trajectories, enabling it to navigate and synthesize information on demand.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/AggAgent_Parallel_Scaling.pdf

papers.title: Multi-ORFT: Stable Online Reinforcement Fine-Tuning for Multi-Agent Diffusion Planning
papers.abstract: We present Multi-ORFT, which couples scene-conditioned diffusion pre-training with stable online reinforcement post-training. The planner uses inter-agent self-attention, cross-attention, and scene conditioning to improve scene consistency.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/Multi_ORFT_Diffusion_Planning.pdf

papers.title: MetaGPT: Meta Programming for Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework
papers.abstract: MetaGPT takes a one line requirement as input and outputs user stories, competitive analysis, requirements, data structures, APIs, documents, etc. Internally, MetaGPT includes product managers, architects, project managers, engineers. It provides the entire process of a software company along with carefully orchestrated SOPs.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/MetaGPT_Multi_Agent.pdf

papers.title: Reflexion: Self-Reflective Agents with Test-Time Self-Improvement
papers.abstract: We introduce Reflexion, an approach for teaching LLM agents to learn from prior failures through verbal reinforcement. Reflexion agents maintain a reflective memory of past experiences to improve decision-making on subsequent attempts.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/03_LLM工具与智能体记忆/Reflexion_Self_Reflective_Agents.pdf

papers.title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
papers.abstract: We present computational agents that simulate believable human behavior. Generative Agents wake up, cook breakfast, and head to work; artists paint, authors write, and they form opinions, notice each other, and initiate conversations.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/03_LLM工具与智能体记忆/Generative_Agents_Human_Behavior.pdf

papers.title: ToolLLM: Facilitating Large Language Models to Master 16000+ Real-world APIs
papers.abstract: Despite the advancements of LLMs, most still struggle to effectively use tools via API calls. We introduce ToolLLM, a general tool-use framework including data construction, model training, and evaluation.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/04_AI智能体与机器人协作2025/ToolLLM_API_Framework.pdf

papers.title: Gorilla: Large Language Model Connected with Massive APIs
papers.abstract: We present Gorilla, a finetuned LLaMA-based model that surpasses GPT-4 on writing API calls. With document retrieval, Gorilla demonstrates strong generalization to novel APIs and handles API updates.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/04_AI智能体与机器人协作2025/Gorilla_API_LLM.pdf

papers.title: Lumos: Empowering Collaborative Agent Systems with Dynamic Planning
papers.abstract: Lumos is a framework for training collaborative, embodied agents with dynamic planning capabilities. It enables agents to plan complex tasks through coordinated multi-agent interactions.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/Lumos_Collaborative_Agents.pdf

papers.title: AgentTuning: Enabling Generalized Agent Abilities for LLMs
papers.abstract: We present AgentTuning, a method to enable LLMs with generalized agent abilities through instruction tuning. We construct AgentInstruct, a dataset containing high-quality interaction trajectories for agent tasks.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/04_AI智能体与机器人协作2025/AgentTuning_Generalized_Agents.pdf

papers.title: WebArena: A Realistic Web Environment for Building Autonomous Agents
papers.abstract: We introduce WebArena, a realistic, standalone web environment for building autonomous agents. WebArena creates a sandbox with four popular web categories: e-commerce, social forums, software development, and content management.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/04_AI智能体与机器人协作2025/WebArena_Autonomous_Agents.pdf

papers.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
papers.abstract: We introduce Voyager, the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent that explores the world, acquires diverse skills, and makes novel discoveries without human intervention.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/03_LLM工具与智能体记忆/Voyager_Lifelong_Learning_Agent.pdf

papers.title: SwiftSage: Generating Fast and Slow Thinking for LLM Agents
papers.abstract: We propose SwiftSage, a novel agent framework that combines fast and slow thinking for interactive decision making. The fast model handles routine decisions while the slow model tackles complex reasoning.
key_contributions: Fast and slow thinking for agents
papers.local_path: 论文收集/04_AI智能体与机器人协作2025/SwiftSage_Fast_Slow_Thinking.pdf

papers.title: AdaRubric: Task-Adaptive Rubrics for LLM Agent Evaluation
papers.abstract: We present ADARUBRIC, which generates task-specific evaluation rubrics on the fly from task descriptions. On WebArena and ToolBench, ADARUBRIC achieves Pearson r=0.79 human correlation.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/04_AI智能体与机器人协作2025/AdaRubric_Task_Adaptive.pdf

papers.title: ShardMemo: Masked MoE Routing for Sharded Agentic LLM Memory
papers.abstract: We present ShardMemo, a budgeted tiered memory service with Tier A per-agent working state, Tier B sharded evidence with shard-local ANN indexes, and Tier C versioned skill library.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/03_LLM工具与智能体记忆/ShardMemo_Sharded_Memory.pdf

papers.title: SemaClaw: A Step Towards General-Purpose Personal AI Agents
papers.abstract: We present SemaClaw, an open-source multi-agent application framework. Our primary contributions include a DAG-based two-phase hybrid agent team orchestration method, a PermissionBridge behavioral safety system, and a three-tier context management architecture.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/SemaClaw_Personal_AI_Agents.pdf

papers.title: λ_A: A Typed Lambda Calculus for LLM Agent Composition
papers.abstract: We present λ_A, a typed lambda calculus for agent composition that extends simply-typed lambda calculus with oracle calls, bounded fixpoints, probabilistic choice, and mutable environments.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/Lambda_Calculus_Agent_Composition.pdf

papers.title: Governance by Design: A Parsonian Institutional Architecture for Internet-Wide Agent Societies
papers.abstract: We argue that governing internet-wide agent societies requires institutional design. Applying Talcott Parsons' AGIL framework, we derive a prescriptive sixteen-cell institutional architecture for internet-wide agent governance.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/Governance_Agent_Societies.pdf

papers.title: EmoMAS: Emotion-Aware Multi-Agent System for High-Stakes Edge-Deployable Negotiation
papers.abstract: We introduce EmoMAS, a Bayesian multi-agent framework that transforms emotional decision-making from reactive to strategic. EmoMAS leverages a Bayesian orchestrator to coordinate three specialized agents.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/EmoMAS_Emotion_Aware_MAS.pdf

papers.title: MA-IDS: Multi-Agent RAG Framework for IoT Network Intrusion Detection
papers.abstract: We propose MA-IDS, a Multi-Agent Intrusion Detection System that combines LLMs with Retrieval Augmented Generation for reasoning-driven intrusion detection with a persistent Experience Library.
papers.local_path: 论文收集/02_多Agent协作/MA_IDS_Intrusion_Detection.pdf
